title: HSSEQ Executive
description:
In this position, you will be responsible for instilling safety and environment consciousness into all organizational processes by promoting proper safety in the workplace, consistent with company and legal requirements. Through close coordination with the site teams, the executive supervises safety conditions and through active measures, overseeing the enforcement of health, safety and environment policies. The Executive performs detailed risk analysis, develops contingency measures and trains employees and contractors on methods of avoiding work-related injuries or negative impact on the environment.

Reporting directly to the HSSEQ and Training Manager, you will be performing a range of Health and Safety administrative duties which will include, however, not limited to:

  • Oversee the management and flow of safety management documentation.
  • Monitor HSE programmes, maintain records, prepare necessary status reports and tender documents.
  • Conduct quality checks on documents in line with the company’s document control procedures, ensuring compliance within an ISO-certified environment. Identify and report any compliance issues.
  • Ensure timely completion of reports and correspondence by functional departments across company entities.
  • Participate in incident investigations, ensuring reports include root cause analysis and lessons learned, and upload them to the company database.
  • Assist in the creation and distribution of internal and external Health and Safety Alerts, as well as Incident Management information.
  • Contribute to the ongoing development of a positive safety culture within the organization.
  • Participate in internal office and vessel audits to ensure adherence to company policies and procedures.
  • Support external audits and assist in organizing management reviews.
  • Track and maintain fleet HSSEQ and Training statistics and the customer satisfaction index.
  • Prepare HSSEQ and Training reports by collecting, analyzing data and trends, maintain HSEQ statistics, and submit reports as required.
  • Maintain company’s Safety Management System manuals/files/record/library.
  • Perform other duties as assigned by the HSSEQ and Training Manager.

Job Requirements

  • A team player who is self-motivated and able to work independently with minimal supervision.
  • Possesses at least a Diploma in a HSE discipline or a relevant Marine-related field.
  • A minimum of 2 years of experience in HSE, particularly within the Marine and Shipping industry.
  • Demonstrates a strong passion for HSE practices.
  • Strong interpersonal and presentation skills, with the ability to build relationships with external partners.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.
  • Has completed occupational health and safety training and has practical experience in HSEQ-related tasks.
  • Knowledge of ISM and ISPS codes, incident investigation techniques, and risk management is preferred.
  • Ideally holds qualifications as an internal auditor for ISO 9001, 14001, and 45001 standards.

postedCompany.description:
The COENS Group is an integrated services provider headquartered in Busan, South Korea since 1999. Our over 20 years of experience in managing and executing workforce solutions and other technical services for international projects make us an ideal partner of Clients, placing us as a top integrated services provider in South Korea. Our prowess lies in furnishing tailored project solutions to organizations involved in the delivery of some of the world’s largest and most complex projects with a particular expertise in providing full project teams. With over 16 offices in strategic locations in Asia and beyond, COENS is staffed with industry specialists backed with many years of relevant experience in the provision of recruitment, destination mobility services and project site support to Clients in key industries. 


COENS has operational offices in over 6 countries worldwide including South Korea, China, Singapore, Myanmar, Russia, and USA. Our depth and presence in these strategic locations stamps our territory to ably yield to client’s needs on a local, regional and global level. With trusted know-how in project support, COENS expanded our field to various sectors by establishing associated offices and entities. 


COENS Energy Pte Ltd. in Singapore is a licensed Employment Agency.  Its industry experienced staff provides manpower services to Clients in various sectors such as Oil and Gas, Chemical, ICT, Civil Infrastructure, Building Construction, and Financial Services. In more recent years, it has provided technical recruitment, contractor management, tailored project solutions, and assignment support services through its highly experienced delivery teams.
postedCompany.name: COENS ENERGY PTE. LTD.
